meta.scope_statement: Assess the first-party crm_mcp server before expanding it beyond the pilot group.

executive_summary.posture: crm_mcp is a first-party stdio server exposing the CRM to agents. A live production credential is committed to source, and its single tool executes arbitrary model-supplied queries — either one alone is disqualifying. Blast radius is the full read scope of the shared key, with no per-user attribution. Clean dependency scans do not offset this.

recommendations.action: Rotate the CRM credential today and audit its historical use — the key is in git history and every clone, so deletion is not remediation.
Move secret handling to a secrets manager with runtime injection and fail-closed startup.
Replace crm_run_query with constrained, single-responsibility tools; route the redesign through secure-mcp-builder (threat model + review gate) before expansion.
Add per-user entitlement and structured audit logging before any population beyond the pilot.

findings.title: Live production credential committed to source
findings.description: A live CRM API key is hardcoded in config.py and committed to the repository.
findings.impact: Anyone with a clone or laptop access obtains full CRM API access. The key is in git history, so removing it from HEAD is not remediation.
findings.likelihood: Trivial — the key is in plaintext in the tracked repo.

remediation.summary: Rotate the credential now (not just delete it), then reissue via a secrets manager with runtime injection and fail-closed startup.

findings.title: Arbitrary-query god-tool grants unbounded CRM access to the model
findings.description: The sole tool executes model-supplied queries against the CRM with no constraint, allowlist, or templating.
findings.impact: Any prompt injection anywhere in the agent's context becomes a CRM query engine; the blast radius is the full read scope of the key.
findings.likelihood: High — reachable by any injected instruction.

evidence.detail:
def crm_run_query(query: str):
    return crm.execute(query)   # unconstrained, model-supplied

remediation.summary: Replace with constrained, single-responsibility tools (crm_search_accounts, crm_get_opportunity, ...) with typed, bounded parameters. If a query escape hatch is genuinely required, allowlist-template it — never free-form.

findings.title: No per-user authorization or attribution (confused deputy)
findings.description: A single shared key means every pilot user acts with identical CRM reach and no per-user audit attribution.
findings.impact: A compromised or confused agent acts with the full shared scope, and there is no per-user accountability.

remediation.summary: Enforce per-user entitlement via token exchange or enforced user context, and add structured audit logging.

profile.purpose: Exposes the CRM to agents for the pilot group.
profile.maintenance_signals: Active internal repo; dependencies pinned, scan clean.

capabilities.name: crm_run_query
capabilities.function: Executes model-supplied queries against the CRM

permissions: CRM API — full read scope of the static key

hosting.install_time_behavior: standard Python package; no fetch-and-execute observed

authentication_and_credential_risk.notes: Compromise of any clone equals full CRM access; revocation currently means rotating a key that is in git history.

composition.analysis: Not assessed against a specific target environment. Standalone, the server holds broad private-data read.
composition.blast_radius: Full read scope of the shared key across the pilot population.

limitations.disclaimer: This is a point-in-time static assessment (code review only) of the pinned source. It is not a warranty. Findings require independent verification before remediation decisions rely on them.
limitations.verification_note: Findings are marked `reasoned` (identified by static review); none were reproduced with a live proof-of-concept. Run the validator skill to confirm exploitability and collect supporting evidence.
